---
name: Architecture pattern proposal
about: Propose a new privacy-preserving or AI-resilient architecture pattern for the architectures/ folder
title: "docs(arch): <pattern name>"
labels: architecture, documentation
assignees: zjywy0228
---

## Pattern name

<!-- Short name, e.g. "Federated-Query with Output Perturbation" -->

## Problem / use-case

<!-- What institution type, data type, or regulatory context motivates this pattern?
     Examples: biomedical cohort analytics, scientific instrument data, federated hospital networks. -->

## Threat model

<!-- What raw-data exposure or AI-leakage risks does this pattern address?
     Reference specific NIST AI RMF or CISA AI Data Security concerns if applicable. -->

## Proposed components

<!-- Sketch the main building blocks (e.g. query broker, differential-privacy layer, audit log, access-control policy). -->

## Data-flow summary

<!-- One paragraph or a rough mermaid diagram describing data movement and where privacy controls apply. -->

## Federal alignment

| Framework | Relevant controls |
|---|---|
| NIST AI RMF | |
| NIST Privacy Framework | |
| NIST CSF 2.0 | |
| CISA AI Data Security | |

## References

<!-- Real DOIs or URLs only. Do not invent references. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Pattern is distinct from existing ones in `architectures/`
- [ ] All references are real and publicly accessible
- [ ] No institutional data, PHI, or credentials are included
